updates.tweet_text: @KnightTrader999 Have to remember that yesteday's 7511 long wasn't my first long. I'd been long since the Sunday 6pm 7421 Failed Breakdown, so my cost basis isn't at 7516 its much lower. Its just a 10% runner though so it doesn't really matter either way
updates.notes: Trade management/hindsight reply: references 7511 and 7421 (both in morning plan) as prior entries, no new actionable level named.

updates.tweet_text: @KnightTrader999 The 2:50PM one was perfectly fine, nothing wrong with that entry. I was referring to the 12:50PM one in the newsletter, which I mentioned specifically

updates.tweet_text:
Short session for #ES_F. Late day yesterday we finally got volatility (sell to 7483 support) & that means trades. I wrote at 3:10PM that 7521 must reclaim to rally. Shortly after, 21 reclaimed and longs triggered. +40

Plan: Ride runner. 7521/7511=supports. 7567, 7578, 7597 next https://t.co/oSGyQgmSWK
updates.notes: 7567 named as 'next' resistance target not in morning plan. 7521, 7511, 7578, 7597 are all morning-plan levels (reaffirmations).

updates.tweet_text: @fiveinchhammer @KnightTrader999 Yes runner stops out below the prior days low, but if my entry is much lower as it was here and I like the structure I am fairly loose with this rule. It is always only a 10% runner for me (tiny)  with a large embedded profit. Would often take price going 50-100 points below my
updates.notes: Reply explaining runner stop management philosophy; no specific actionable price level named, only general distance commentary (50-100 points).
